focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Betts suggests Janine trick Nick into seeing a doctor about his lumps by having Dr. St. Luc visit unexpectedly, while Benda remains shyly in the background, unnoticed until Betts briefly acknowledges her.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Concerned wife seeking medical help for husband
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Agrees to Betts's plan to have Dr. St. Luc visit Nick unexpectedly for a medical check
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Arrange a surprise doctor visit for Nick's lumps
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Has accepted a deception plan to get Nick examined by a doctor
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the plan will succeed and what the diagnosis will be
evidence_quotes: JANINE He hates doctors. Doctors and lawyers. He never goes to doctors.
JANINE (not displeased with the idea) He'll be really mad.

status_updates: Willing to use deception for Nick's health
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Proactive in addressing Nick's health concerns based on advice

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: St. Luc remains focused on Tudor's file, visibly troubled, while Forsythe informs him that Mrs. Ementhal is ready; he acknowledges but continues his review.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Arranges doctor's home visit for husband
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Asks Dr. St. Luc to examine Nick at home and he agrees
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Secure Dr. St. Luc's agreement to visit Nick at home
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Home visit scheduled for Nick at 9:30-10:00 PM
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Nick's response to the scheduled examination
evidence_quotes: You'll come up and take a look at him?
If he can't make it down here... sure. That's what I'm here for.
9.30, say 10.00. OK? Not too late?
That's great, Doctor. Thanks.

relation_updates: Establishes trust with Dr. St. Luc

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Janine buys a Vogue magazine at a grocery store but leaves without purchasing food, feeling repulsed by the thought of cooking or eating.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Janine in the grocery store
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Repulsed by food, buys only a magazine
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Leaves the store with only a magazine
evidence_quotes: but somehow the thought of cooking or even eating repulses her,
and she leaves without buying anything but the magazine.

status_updates: Loss of appetite, aversion to cooking
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Repulsed by the thought of cooking or eating

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tudor, hiding strange lumps under his skin, rebuffs Janine's concerned attempts to help, causing her to leave in tears as he smiles mysteriously.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Wife attempting to assist husband with his health issue
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Janine enters bedroom to check on Tudor, is told to leave, and exits in tears after frustration
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To examine and assist with Tudor's stomach lumps
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Leaves the bedroom after Tudor's refusal
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Tudor's mysterious condition and his refusal of assistance
evidence_quotes: JANINE (tenderly, but with caution, as though expecting a blow) Nick, does your stomach hurt? Can I see those bumps on your tummy, can I?
TUDOR Go away. Leave me alone.
JANINE (frustrated) Oh, why won't you let me help you?
She turns and walks angrily out of the room, slamming the door behind her.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Caring but emotionally distressed by rejection

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Janine, distressed, begins undressing Tudor, who pleads for intimacy; she feels lumps on his abdomen, reacting with horror, while he insists she continue as his wife.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Wife in forced intimate situation
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Discovers abnormal lumps on Tudor's abdomen, reacts with horror

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Janine watches in terror as multiple tentacles force Tudor's mouth open, revealing a creature emerging from his body; she flees, stumbling to the hallway.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Observer of Tudor's abnormal physical manifestation
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Discovers a twitching, insect-like appendage in Tudor's mouth and reacts with protective horror
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: In the bedroom, covering her mouth in fear after seeing the entity
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Nature of the entity in Tudor's mouth and her immediate safety
evidence_quotes: The black thing, like the tip of an insect's leg, twitches.
Janine jerks back in horror, her hands, balled into fists, cover her own mouth as though to protect it from whatever occupies Tudor's mouth.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Janine rushes to Betts's apartment, crying and stumbling, and opens the door without hesitation.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Running to Betts's apartment for refuge
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Runs sobbing and stumbling to Betts's apartment
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Opens Betts's door
evidence_quotes: Janine runs down a hallway that leads to Betts's apartment, sobbing and stumbling.
She gets to Betts's door and opens it without hesitation.

relation_updates: Seeks refuge with Betts, indicating trust

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Betts kisses Janine passionately, then forces a parasite from her own throat into Janine's mouth, overwhelming her as she gags and fails to escape.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Victim of forced parasite transmission by Betts
timeline_nodes.salient_development: During a forced kiss, Betts transfers a parasite from her throat into Janine's mouth
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Gagging and infected, physically overpowered and unable to escape
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Immediate physical effects of the parasite and the broken trust with Betts
evidence_quotes: Betts suddenly opens her mouth wide and presses her lips savagely against Janine's.
The parasite begins to force its way into Janine's mouth and down her throat.
Janine gags and tries to pull away, but too late.

relation_updates: Relationship with Betts shifts to attacker-victim

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: St. Luc is forced underwater by Betts and Janine; a parasite transfers from Janine to him during a violent kiss, causing grotesque physical transformation before he sinks, calm, amid chaotic onlookers joining the pool.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Infector who transmits parasite to St. Luc
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Forcibly kisses St. Luc, causing parasite to transfer from her to him
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Drifting calmly in the pool after the parasite transfer
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Janine's physical state after losing the parasite
evidence_quotes: a laughing Janine splashes over to him and fastens her mouth to his.
Janine's throat ripples and swells, her cheeks billow as a parasite swarms upwards from deep within her body.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Janine begins as a concerned wife who actively arranges medical help for her husband Nick. She experiences emotional distress and horror upon discovering Nick's transformation, leading her to flee to her friend Betts. Betrayed and infected by Betts, Janine ultimately becomes an infector herself, transmitting a parasite to Dr. St. Luc.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rollo, after sharing his emotional story, asks St. Luc to consider working together; St. Luc agrees to think about it before leaving.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: General practitioner invited to collaborate on controversial research
timeline_nodes.salient_development: St. Luc is asked by Rollo to join the research project and learns of Hobbes's sexual misconduct with a twelve-year-old girl named Annabelle.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: St. Luc has heard Rollo's proposal and the disturbing details about Annabelle, and is aware of her photo in the lab, but has not committed to joining.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether St. Luc will join the project and the full details of Hobbes's relationship with Annabelle.
evidence_quotes: ROLLO Rog, I gotta talk serious to you. Really. Listen. Ya listening? OK. I want you to come into this with me.
ST. LUC (a bit uncomfortable being put on the spot) Rollo, you know me. Once a GP, always a GP.
He glances at a picture of Annabelle stuck in a corner, which St. Luc just notices for the first time.

relation_updates: Rollo proposes partnership to St. Luc
St. Luc learns of Hobbes's misconduct with Annabelle
status_updates: St. Luc becomes aware of the photo of Annabelle
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A cautious general practitioner reluctant to leave his medical practice for research.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: St. Luc remains focused on Tudor's file, visibly troubled, while Forsythe informs him that Mrs. Ementhal is ready; he acknowledges but continues his review.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Doctor reviewing Tudor's medical file in his clinic office
timeline_nodes.salient_development: St. Luc reviews Tudor's file, questions the cancer diagnosis, and agrees to a late-night home visit.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To examine Tudor at home and assess his symptoms.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: St. Luc has scheduled a home visit for 9:30-10:00 p.m. and continues studying Tudor's file with concern.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: What is troubling St. Luc about Tudor's file, and what will he discover during the home visit?
evidence_quotes: I just can't see cancer developing that fast, Janine, not the way you've described it.
If he can't make it down here... sure. That's what I'm here for.
St. Luc keeps staring at Tudor's file, shifts something from one side of the folder to the other. Something bothers him.

relation_updates: Janine trusts St. Luc to visit her husband at home
status_updates: St. Luc takes on a new patient case (Tudor)

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dr. St. Luc examines Mr. Parkins, who has abdominal lumps from a relationship with Annabelle Horsefield, and sends him for X-rays to investigate further, avoiding immediate surgery.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Doctor investigating a medical mystery
timeline_nodes.salient_development: St. Luc examines Parkins' abdominal lumps, learns they may have been transmitted from Annabelle Horsefield, and orders X-rays to investigate further.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To identify the nature of Parkins' lumps through X-rays.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: St. Luc has referred Parkins for X-rays and is considering the connection to Annabelle Horsefield.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The exact nature of the lumps and their connection to Annabelle Horsefield.
evidence_quotes: St. Luc presses gently around Parkins' abdomen in the area of the navel.
St. Luc stops pressing and takes up his stethoscope. He begins to percuss the old man's abdomen.
Mr. Parkins, what makes you think you caught these lumps of yours from a young lady?
I'm going to send you to the hospital to have a few X-rays taken.

relation_updates: Parkins reveals his relationship with Annabelle and the origin of his lumps
status_updates: St. Luc gains new information about Annabelle's condition

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rollo, Hobbes's partner, received his personal and medical papers from Hobbes's mother and discovered evidence that Hobbes had been deceiving everyone, including the university, foundations, and private labs.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Listener to Rollo's disclosure about Hobbes's deception
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Hears Rollo explain that Hobbes was deceiving the university, foundations, private labs, and others based on his papers
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Informed of Hobbes's fraudulent activities as disclosed by Rollo
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How Hobbes's fraud impacts current work and trust
evidence_quotes: Listen, Rog. I knew Hobbes was funny, you know? I told you that. But I didn't really know just how funny he was.

relation_updates: Gains new understanding of Hobbes's actions via Rollo
status_updates: Becomes aware of Hobbes's fraudulent activities

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: St. Luc finds unidentified pathogenic abdominal growths in multiple individuals linked to Annabelle, with Forsythe potentially at risk, as Hobbes may have been unaware of her connections.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Investigator informing Rollo about victims with abdominal growths linked to Annabelle
timeline_nodes.salient_development: St. Luc states that three or four men linked to Annabelle have unidentified abdominal growths and identifies Forsythe as a potential next victim
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To share findings on the abdominal growths and assess Forsythe's risk
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: St. Luc has informed Rollo about the pattern of abdominal growths and noted Forsythe's potential involvement
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The unidentified pathogenic abdominal growths and their cause
evidence_quotes: He toys with the Velakofsky file, which contains abdominal X-rays showing dark, blurred masses inside the abdominal cavity.

status_updates: Has a list of 3-4 victims with abdominal growths

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Forsythe believes she killed her attacker; St. Luc decides to investigate, taking her along as they head to her place to check if the incident is linked to Hobbes's 'compressed evolution' theory.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Doctor investigating a reported attack in relation to his research
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Decides to investigate Forsythe's attack at her apartment, suspecting a link to Hobbes's compressed evolution theory, and takes her along
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To check if the attacker is still present and if the incident is connected to the parasite theory
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Departing with Forsythe to her apartment to investigate
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the attacker is still alive and if the attack is related to the compressed evolution theory
evidence_quotes: St. Luc is reading a section of one of Hobbes's medical papers in preparation for examining Tudor later on.
St. Luc hesitates for a second, then grabs his black leather doctor's bag.

status_updates: Shifts from passive reading to active investigation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: St. Luc fights off a large man attacking him, kills him with a poker, then flees in shock, bleeding and dazed, after realizing what he's done.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Investigator attacked while examining evidence, kills attacker in self-defense
timeline_nodes.salient_development: During a struggle with an intruder, St. Luc kills the man using a poker and kicks, then reacts with shock
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Flee the incinerator room after the fatal fight
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Shocked, bleeding, and staggering up the basement steps
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Horrified by the killing and physically injured, fleeing the scene
evidence_quotes: St. Luc leaps to his feet and begins kicking the man in the head.
he suddenly stops, drops the poker, and stares at the body in horrified disbelief.
himself scratched and bleeding, and staggers up the basement steps.

relation_updates: Violent confrontation with hairy muscular man

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: St. Luc gags and carries Forsythe after her mouth reveals parasitic tentacles; he knocks down a woman entering the gym and flees with Forsythe struggling.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Decision-maker facing infection revelation
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Discovers Forsythe is infected when parasite tentacles emerge from her mouth, gags her with strips torn from her blouse, throws her over his shoulder, knocks down a woman entering the gym, and flees
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Escape with gagged Forsythe while preventing parasite transmission
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Fleeing the gym with Forsythe gagged and struggling over his shoulder
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to handle infected Forsythe and continue escape to find Rollo and police
evidence_quotes: St. Luc hesitates only for an instant, then rips a strip from her blouse, balls it up, and shoves it into her mouth.
St. Luc rises, throws Forsythe over his shoulder and begins to step toward the door of the gym.
St. Luc rushes at the woman, knocking her over.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Acts decisively under threat, prioritizing containment and escape over hesitation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: St. Luc is forced underwater by Betts and Janine; a parasite transfers from Janine to him during a violent kiss, causing grotesque physical transformation before he sinks, calm, amid chaotic onlookers joining the pool.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Infected victim in ritualistic attack
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Forcibly infected by a parasite transferred from Janine during a forced kiss, causing grotesque physical swelling and bleeding.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Calm and passive after infection, drifting underwater with Janine.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The nature and extent of his physical transformation and identity change.
evidence_quotes: ST. LUC Have you seen the police? I'm Dr. St. Luc. Have you seen the police? Have they come?
Janine and St. Luc drift apart, now completely calm, as residents splash and swim, kick and embrace.

relation_updates: Adversarial interaction with Betts, Janine, and Spergazzi
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Dr. St. Luc, a medical professional seeking police, now infected and subdued.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: St. Luc leads a convoy of cars from a garage atop Starliner Towers, accelerating into the neon-lit city with others following in a stream of lights.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Leader of the convoy escaping Starliner Towers
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Drives the first car out of the garage ramp, leading a line of following cars into the city streets.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Escape Starliner Towers by leading the convoy to safety.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Driving into the neon-lit metropolis with cars following in a stream of lights.
evidence_quotes: One car surges up the ramp ahead of all the others, stopping at the top.
St. Luc sees all the other cars lining up behind him, lights blazing.
St. Luc smiles, then steps on the accelerator.
car after car follows him.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sleek and exuberant, with a raised collar and scarf hiding most of his scars.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: St. Luc, a doctor at Starliner Towers, is recruited by Rollo Linsky for a controversial project and learns of his deceased colleague Hobbes's misconduct. He investigates a series of abdominal growths linked to Annabelle Horsefield, uncovering a pattern of victims and confronting infected individuals. His actions escalate to violence and self-defense, culminating in his own infection and transformation, after which he leads a convoy of residents out of the building.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tudor takes a key from his wallet and presses the top floor button as the elevator ascends, while Merrick describes recessed, individually lit apartment entrances.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Visitor to top floor apartment with a key
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After Merrick and the Svibens exit the elevator, Tudor presses the top floor button and retrieves a key from his wallet.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To access the top floor apartment
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: In the elevator ascending to the top floor, holding a key
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The purpose of his visit to the apartment
evidence_quotes: Once the door has closed, Tudor presses the button for the top floor.
he takes out his wallet
removes a key from a zippered compartment.

relation_updates: Implies a secret relationship with Annabelle.
status_updates: In elevator heading to top floor.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Acts discreetly by waiting for the elevator to empty before using the key

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tudor unlocks and enters Annabelle's apartment quietly, closing the door behind him to avoid being seen by anyone who might recognize him.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Person entering Annabelle's apartment with a key while avoiding detection
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tudor uses a key to unlock Annabelle's apartment, knocks gently, enters after a pause, and closes the door to prevent being seen.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To enter Annabelle's apartment without being detected
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Inside Annabelle's apartment with the door closed
evidence_quotes: Tudor fits the key into the lock of Annabelle's apartment.
He knocks gently
opens the door
closes the door behind him

relation_updates: Confirms secret access to Annabelle's living space.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tudor confirms Annabelle's identity with surgical clip and neck bruises, reacts physically, then exits the apartment, taking his attaché case and closing the door.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Discoverer of double murder
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tudor enters Annabelle's apartment and finds both bodies: Hobbes in fetal position with a scalpel in his neck, and Annabelle on the dining table with a surgical clip on her lips and bruises on her neck.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Tudor staggers from the apartment, taking his attaché case and closing the door behind him.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Who killed Annabelle and Hobbes? Is Tudor in danger?
evidence_quotes: Annabelle's corpse is still smoking where it lies on the dining-room table.
It is definitely Annabelle, eyes still staring, surgical clip still attached to her lips, purple bruises on her neck.

status_updates: Aware of Annabelle's and Hobbes's deaths.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Nick Tudor hides his discomfort from Janine, feigning normalcy while secretly checking his stomach for issues after she leaves.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Hiding abdominal pain from Janine
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Doubles over in pain, presses his navel for lumps, and conceals discomfort when Janine inquires.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: After Janine leaves, he turns off the water-pick and examines his stomach again.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The cause of his abdominal pain and whether he will seek treatment remain unknown.
evidence_quotes: Suddenly, he doubles over in a soundless spasm of pain.
Tudor tries to cover up, grabs the water-pick, and begins to work on his teeth again, hiding the occasional twinge that hits him.
Tudor waits for a second, turns off the water-pick, then checks out his stomach again.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tudor suffers a violent episode, vomiting blood-streaked slime into the bathtub before collapsing, exhausted and bleeding, on the bathroom floor.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Suffering a violent health crisis alone in his apartment
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Experiences a severe physical episode, vomiting blood and collapsing on the bathroom floor
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Collapsed on the bathroom floor, mouth bloody, after vomiting blood-streaked slime into the bathtub
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The cause and consequences of this health crisis are unknown
evidence_quotes: Tudor suddenly contracts into the fetal position, spilling his drink on to the floor.
He gags and vomits into the tub and collapses, exhausted, on the floor, mouth bloody.
In the tub, a trail of blood-streaked slime leads into the drain.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tudor, hiding strange lumps under his skin, rebuffs Janine's concerned attempts to help, causing her to leave in tears as he smiles mysteriously.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Tudor hides his lumps from Janine and rejects her help.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tudor interacts affectionately with the lumps under his skin, treating them like a pet, while refusing Janine's attempts to assist him.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Alone in bed, smiling and murmuring to the lumps after Janine leaves.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Janine is upset and has left, and Tudor's condition with the lumps continues.
evidence_quotes: TUDOR (delirious, voice strained, whispering) Come here, boy. Here, boy, here.
TUDOR Go away. Leave me alone.
Tudor rolls over slowly on to his back, eyes wide and shining, smiling again. TUDOR (murmuring) Attaboy, attaboy.

relation_updates: Janine attempts to help but is rejected, leading to emotional conflict and her departure in tears.
status_updates: Tudor has lumps under his skin that he interacts with deliriously.
He is in a weak, sweaty state but smiles maniacally.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Janine, distressed, begins undressing Tudor, who pleads for intimacy; she feels lumps on his abdomen, reacting with horror, while he insists she continue as his wife.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Sick husband pleading for intimacy with his wife
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Janine feels abnormal lumps on Tudor's abdomen while reluctantly caressing him, pulling away in horror; Tudor continues pleading for her to make love to him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To have Janine make love to him.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Still in bed, pleading with Janine after she recoils from feeling his lumps.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Nature of Tudor's illness and whether Janine will continue.
evidence_quotes: She can now see that Tudor, although pale and sickly, is smiling ecstatically.
Janine's hand sweeps across Tudor's abdomen. She pulls her hand away, startled, obviously having just felt a few of Tudor's lumps.
She looks up at Tudor's face with a mixture of horror and wonder in her eyes.
TUDOR (pleading) You're my wife, Janine. Please make love to me.

status_updates: Has abnormal lumps on abdomen indicating illness.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Pale and sickly but smiling ecstatically, desperate for physical connection.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rollo, disfigured and horrified by his reflection, is attacked by Tudor, who leaps up and violently beats him with pliers, a piece of parasite falling onto Rollo's face as he drools.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Desperate defender of parasites
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After pleading fails, Tudor violently attacks Rollo to stop him from destroying parasites.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Violently attacking Rollo on the floor with pliers.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ongoing struggle with Rollo over the parasites.
evidence_quotes: He starts to paw Rollo, feebly trying to prevent him from further mutilating the parasites.
Tudor begins to shove the piece of flesh back down his throat.
Without warning, Tudor leaps up at Rollo with insane energy and bowls him over, pliers in hand.

relation_updates: Adversarial conflict with Rollo intensifies through physical violence.
status_updates: Gains and uses pliers as a weapon.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Tudor secretly visits Annabelle's apartment and discovers her murder, initiating a chain of events. He develops a mysterious illness marked by abdominal pain and lumps, which he hides from his wife Janine. As his condition deteriorates, he embraces the parasitic entities inside him, leading to violent transformations and aggressive defense of the parasites.
